description: Article-level inclusion/exclusion decision for curated exports.
Boolean flag for conservative model-ready rows.
Semicolon-separated reasons why a row is not model-ready, or ready.
Selected primary target according to target priority.
Numeric value of the selected primary target.
Unit of the selected primary target.
Suggested optimization direction for the target.
PubChem enrichment status: matched, no_match, error, not_queried, or unknown_payload.
PubChem canonical/connectivity SMILES for descriptor generation when available.
PubChem compound identifier for matched small molecules.
UniProt enrichment status: matched, no_match, error, not_queried, or unknown_payload.
Whether the UniProt match is directly usable or needs accession/species review.
UniProt accession for matched protein query.
Whether a protein sequence was returned and can be used for sequence embeddings.
Whitelisted nested record path proposed for second-pass completion.
Origin of the patch value; only observed_fulltext patches may be applied to experimental records.
Patch validation/application result such as applied, eligible_not_applied, or rejected_existing_value.
Observed article-level field that could not be safely mapped to one exact record.
Always external_descriptor; sidecar chemistry feature, not an observed experiment field.
Always external_descriptor; sidecar protein feature, not an observed experiment field.
RCSB PDB polymer entity identifiers linked from a QA-usable UniProt accession.
Sidecar descriptor rows considered usable after source-specific QA.
Source table or figure used as evidence.
Material format, such as bulk gel, coating, bead, cryogel, or nanogel.
Post-treatment, washing, conditioning, or storage notes.
Degradation, reuse, storage, or stability notes.
Role of the protein in the experiment.
Reported or inferred protein charge at assay pH.
Matrix, such as buffer, serum, plasma, or crude extract.
Batch, surface, column, SPR/QCM, or other assay mode.
Endpoint, equilibrium, kinetic, dynamic, or unknown.
Single protein, binary, serum, plasma, blood, or other competition context.
Normalized capacity in mg/g if extractable.
Normalized capacity in mg/mL bed if extractable.
Surface adsorption in microgram per square centimeter.
Dynamic binding capacity as reported, including its original units when available.
Selectivity factor as reported for competitive or imprinted systems.

Dissociation constant as reported, including its original units when available.
Mechanism tags extracted from the article.
Whether the outcome value was normalized.
